relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Love is pursued while spiritual gifts are desired, especially prophecy.
Tongues speak mysteries to the Divine.
Prophecy builds, encourages, and consoles humans.
Tongues build the self; prophecy builds the assembly.
Tongues need interpretation so the body is built.
A gift benefits others when it brings revelation, knowledge, prophecy, or teaching.
Undifferentiated sound cannot guide song.
Unclear trumpet sound cannot prepare anyone.
Unclear speech becomes air.
Voices have meaning when heard.
Without shared meaning, people become foreigners to one another.
Zeal for spiritual things must abound toward building the assembly.
The tongue-speaker prays for interpretation.
Spirit may pray while mind remains unfruitful.
Paul wants spirit and mind together in prayer and song.
Thanksgiving needs to be intelligible enough for shared Amen.
Beautiful thanksgiving still fails if the other is not built.
Paul honors tongues from experience.
Five intelligible words can serve more than ten thousand hidden ones.
Be infants in harm, mature in thinking.
Scripture remembers foreign tongues as sign and warning.
Tongues and prophecy function differently for different hearers.
Uninterpreted chaos can make outsiders think madness.
Prophecy can expose and awaken the heart.
Hidden heart-things become manifest, and worship recognizes Divine presence.
Every contribution must serve building up.
Tongues are ordered, limited, and interpreted.
Without interpretation, silence becomes private prayer to the Divine.
Prophecy is spoken and discerned.
Fresh revelation makes room by yielding.
All may prophesy in turn so all learn and are encouraged.
Prophetic spirits are not uncontrollable chaos.
The Divine is peace, not disorder.
The silence text must be handled carefully beside women praying and prophesying earlier.
The stated concern is shame/disruption in assembly learning.
Corinth is challenged: the word did not originate with them alone.
Prophetic and spiritual people must recognize apostolic ordering.
Refusal to know remains refusal.
Desire prophecy, and do not forbid tongues.
Let all things happen beautifully and in order.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 14 puts gifts under love’s architecture: intelligibility, discernment, peace, and building the body. The women-silence lines are preserved but flagged by the tension with women praying and prophesying in chapter 11.
